investigation_summary: U.S. v. Philip Morris USA — Judge Kessler 2006 RICO liability for fraudulently denying health risks, concealing research, and marketing to youth. 1998 MSA $206B+ and public document archive (~14M pages at UCSF). Product causal for ~480k annual U.S. deaths (CDC). Child labor on tobacco leaf documented HRW (Malawi, Zimbabwe).

profile.parent: Philip Morris International (NYSE: PM); historically unified with Altria U.S. until 2008 spin
profile.tax_summary: PMI Swiss domicile — rate advantage vs U.S. Altria ~21–24% effective. Complex brand-licensing IP routing both sides.
profile.legal_summary: 1998 MSA — largest civil settlement; document disclosure obligation. 1999–2006 DOJ RICO — civil remedies; fraud findings re marketing and concealment. Menthol rule fight. IQOS modified risk orders contested in public-health literature. Juul (Altria stake) youth-vaping settlements written down.
profile.labor_summary: Leaf-growing regions: HRW reports on child labor in Malawi/Zimbabwe supply chains.
profile.environmental_summary: Cigarette butts — leading litter item; cellulose-acetate filters persistent. 'Unsmoke' campaigns critiqued as harm-reduction marketing.
profile.political_summary: Among top tobacco lobbying / political spend; menthol rule opposition; FARA filings for foreign activities.
profile.product_health: Combustible cigarettes: causal mortality/morbidity undisputed. IQOS aerosol still delivers addictive nicotine and toxicants — not risk-free per FDA communications. Menthol eases inhalation; disproportionate marketing to Black smokers per CDC summaries.
profile.executive_summary: UCSF industry documents — internal research suppression and youth marketing plans (e.g., Project SCUM archival references).
